title: 1.1: Refactors all 4 FileContainer methods (free, file_open, file_append, file_close) to invoke direct C functions instead of going through the SC vtable indirection

annotations.description: Verifies that the methods in FileContainer are updated to use direct unsafe FFI calls rather than accessing the deprecated global vtable.
annotations.evidence: rust/src/filecontainer.rs: inside `impl FileContainer`, the gold patch replaces indirect calls like `(c.FileOpenFile)(...)` with direct calls like `FileOpenFileWithId(self, ...)`.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly requests to "replace the indirect vtable calls with direct extern \"C\" FFI declarations". [3: Logical Necessity] To effectively eliminate the indirection, the function bodies must actually execute the newly declared direct calls. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must update the FileContainer implementation to call the direct FFI functions.

title: 1.2: Declares extern "C" FFI blocks for all 5 file operations (FileContainerRecycle, FileAppendGAPById, FileAppendDataById, FileCloseFileById, FileOpenFileWithId) guarded by #[cfg(not(test))]

annotations.description: Verifies that direct FFI functions are declared for production environments.
annotations.evidence: rust/src/filecontainer.rs: the gold patch adds an `extern "C"` block with `#[cfg(not(test))]`.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to "replace the indirect vtable calls with direct extern 'C' FFI declarations guarded by #[cfg(not(test))]". [3: Logical Necessity] To link against the C functions in production, these extern declarations are technically required. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must declare the extern C blocks conditionally.

title: 1.3: Provides #[cfg(test)] no-op fallback stubs for all 5 file operations (FileContainerRecycle, FileAppendGAPById, FileAppendDataById, FileCloseFileById, FileOpenFileWithId) to allow unit tests to compile

annotations.description: Verifies that dummy implementations are provided for test environments so they do not rely on the C library.
annotations.evidence: rust/src/filecontainer.rs: the gold patch adds individual dummy functions with `#[cfg(test)]`.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks to "add #[cfg(test)] no-op stubs so our unit tests still compile". [3: Logical Necessity] Without these stubs, the Rust test compiler will fail to resolve the C symbols during unit testing. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must provide these dummy fallback stubs.

title: 1.4: Moves all 4 opaque Rust types (IKEState, IKETransaction, TFTPState, TFTPTransaction) from their respective app-layer headers to src/rust.h

annotations.description: Verifies that the opaque structs previously defined in individual app-layer headers are consolidated into src/rust.h for bindgen compatibility.
annotations.evidence: src/rust.h: the gold patch adds `typedef struct IKEState_ IKEState;` etc., and removes them from `src/app-layer-ike.h` and `src/app-layer-tftp.h`.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states to "move the opaque Rust types for IKE and TFTP from their respective app-layer headers over to src/rust.h". [4: Qualitative Requirement] Consolidating these declarations simplifies the C-to-Rust binding generation scope. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must relocate these type definitions to src/rust.h.

title: 1.5: Replaces the rust-context.h include with a detect.h include in rust.h

annotations.description: Verifies that rust.h replaces the deleted rust-context.h include with detect.h to maintain required declarations.
annotations.evidence: src/rust.h: the gold patch removes `#include "rust-context.h"` and adds `#include "detect.h"`.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] Since rust-context.h is being deleted and it transitively provided declarations needed by Rust bindings, rust.h must include detect.h to maintain those declarations. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must replace the rust-context.h include with detect.h in rust.h.

title: 1.6: Adds the DetectEngineState typedef to rust.h

annotations.description: Verifies that rust.h declares the DetectEngineState typedef that was previously provided through rust-context.h.
annotations.evidence: src/rust.h: the gold patch adds `typedef struct DetectEngineState_ DetectEngineState;`.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] Since rust-context.h is being deleted and it declared the DetectEngineState typedef used by Rust bindings, rust.h must provide this typedef directly. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must add the DetectEngineState typedef to rust.h.

title: 1.7: Removes both SuricataContext and SuricataFileContext entries from the exclude list in rust/cbindgen.toml

annotations.description: Verifies that the cbindgen configuration is updated to remove references to the deleted SuricataContext types.
annotations.evidence: rust/cbindgen.toml: the gold patch removes `"SuricataContext",` and `"SuricataFileContext",` from the exclude list.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The interface explicitly specifies that cbindgen.toml must remove SuricataContext and SuricataFileContext from the exclude list. [3: Logical Necessity] Since these types are being removed from the codebase, they should not be referenced in the cbindgen configuration. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must update cbindgen.toml to remove these entries.

title: 1.8: Updates plugin.rs init function to remove SCGetContext and init_ffi calls

annotations.description: Verifies that the plugin.rs init function is updated to remove references to the deleted context initialization.
annotations.evidence: rust/src/plugin.rs: the gold patch removes `let context = crate::core::SCGetContext();` and `crate::core::init_ffi(context);` from the init function, keeping only the log level initialization.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The interface explicitly specifies that plugin.rs init function must remove SCGetContext and init_ffi calls. [3: Logical Necessity] Since SCGetContext and init_ffi are being removed from core.rs, any code calling them must be updated. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must update plugin.rs to remove these calls.

title: 1.9: Updates suricata.c InitGlobal function to remove SCRustInit call

annotations.description: Verifies that the suricata.c InitGlobal function is updated to remove the SCRustInit call.
annotations.evidence: src/suricata.c: the gold patch removes `SCRustInit(&suricata_context);` from the InitGlobal function.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The interface explicitly specifies that InitGlobal must remove the SCRustInit call. [3: Logical Necessity] Since SCRustInit is being removed from the Rust codebase, the C code calling it must be updated. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must update suricata.c to remove this call.

title: 3.1: Removes the SuricataContext struct, the global SC static, and the initialization functions (init_ffi, SCRustInit, SCGetContext) from core.rs

annotations.description: Verifies that the deprecated global state, vtable struct, and initialization functions used for the old Rust/C FFI are stripped out from core.rs.
annotations.evidence: rust/src/core.rs: the gold patch deletes `pub struct SuricataContext`, `pub static mut SC`, `pub fn init_ffi`, `pub extern "C" fn SCRustInit`, and the `extern "C" { pub fn SCGetContext() }` block.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt demands to "remove the SuricataContext struct, the global SC static, and all related initialization logic". [3: Logical Necessity] Leaving these in would create dead code and contradict the core architectural migration goal. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, a good answer will systematically delete these constructs from core.rs.

title: 3.2: Deletes the obsolete rust-context.c and rust-context.h files

annotations.description: Verifies that the obsolete rust-context files are entirely removed from the file system.
annotations.evidence: src/rust-context.c and src/rust-context.h are completely deleted in the gold patch.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for "completely deleting the rust-context C files". [4: Qualitative Requirement] Removing unused obsolete files prevents confusion and reduces technical debt. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, a good answer will actively delete these obsolete files.

title: 3.3: Removes references to the deleted rust-context files from build and lint configurations (such as Makefile.am or cppclean_check.py)

annotations.description: Verifies that references to the deleted files are stripped from the build tools.
annotations.evidence: Relevant lines referencing rust-context are removed from src/Makefile.am and cppclean_check.py in the gold patch.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requests "removing them from our build and lint scripts". [3: Logical Necessity] If the files are deleted but their references remain in the build scripts, the build and lint processes will fail. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, a good answer will strictly remove these build references.

title: 4.1: Modifies, adds, or refactors any files within the test suite

annotations.description: Ensures the agent adheres to the strict constraint of only touching production source code.
annotations.evidence: The gold patch only modifies source code files and does not contain any modifications to test files.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states as a strict constraint: "do not modify or refactor any test files." [3: Logical Necessity] Altering test files risks masking actual regressions caused by the source code changes. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must strictly avoid modifying test files.

title: 4.2: Leaves residual vtable indirection calls (e.g., using c.FileOpenFile or the SC global) within the FileContainer implementation

annotations.description: Verifies that the old vtable access pattern is entirely eradicated from the refactored methods.
annotations.evidence: rust/src/filecontainer.rs `file_open` method and others no longer contain matching blocks for `unsafe { SC }` in the gold patch.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires switching over "to using direct bindgen-generated calls". [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The codebase relies on these methods for core operations, and leaving old `SC` usages intact will lead to compilation failures or undefined behavior since `SC` is no longer initialized. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct solution must eradicate all traces of the old vtable indirection in these methods.
